commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer security/product judgment on the code-mode hook contract before further code changes; this is not a safe automated repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff changes a security-sensitive hook payload for an exec-named tool without a distinct code-mode discriminator.; Review finding: [P1] Keep code-mode exec distinct from shell exec policies — src/agents/code-mode.ts:824-829 (sha=3b1bc5c4cbb25830aff01679655fd6426bb98962)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a `command` alias for OpenClaw code-mode `exec`, mirrors and reconciles `code`/`command` in `before_tool_call` paths, and updates related tests and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blocker at source level: the hook event carries only `toolName` and `params`, while this PR adds `command` to the same `exec` tool contract. I did not run tests because this review is read-only, but the PR body includes terminal proof for the alias behavior.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer security/product judgment on the code-mode hook contract before further code changes; this is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(code-mode): align outer exec hook params
target.body:
Follow-up to #83387 after #83481 landed the active hook-context plumbing.

## Problem

Code-mode exposes its outer `exec` control tool through the Pi custom-tool adapter. On current `main`, the active hook context is already threaded into that outer dispatch via `toolHookContext`, which gives `before_tool_call` handlers the active agent/session/run identity.

The remaining gap is that the outer code-mode `exec` input is model-facing `code`, while conventional exec-shaped hook policies inspect `command`. That makes it harder for one policy to reason consistently about outer code-mode `exec` and normal exec-shaped dispatches.

## Change and value

This PR preserves the current active hook-context path and aligns the outer code-mode `exec` input contract with exec-shaped policy expectations:

- keeps `code` accepted as the documented model-facing field
- accepts `command` as an alias for code-mode `exec`
- mirrors `code` and `command` in `before_tool_call` hook payloads for marked code-mode control tools only
- reconciles single-alias hook rewrites before execution
- rejects divergent `code` and `command` values before execution
- treats non-string counterpart values as absent so hooks still see executable source under both expected names

## Scope

Affected:
- code-mode users with `before_tool_call` hooks or trusted policies that inspect outer code-mode `exec`
- policy authors who want outer code-mode `exec` to expose an exec-compatible `command` field

Not affected:
- normal shell `exec` behavior
- non-code-mode tools named `exec`
- the existing code-mode `code` field, which remains accepted

## Verification

- Local: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/code-mode-control-tools.ts src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts docs/reference/code-mode.md`
- Local: `git diff --check`
- Local: `timeout 120s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t "outer code-mode exec|single-alias" --reporter verbose` passed: 3 tests passed, 14 skipped
- Remote GCP VM, `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-4`, Node `22.22.2`, pnpm `11.1.0`: install, format check, targeted code-mode alias tests, targeted outer code-mode hook tests, and `pnpm build` all passed
- Review: `timeout 900s codex review --commit HEAD` reported no discrete correctness issues

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Outer code-mode `exec` now exposes the executable source under both the model-facing `code` field and the exec-shaped `command` alias when `before_tool_call` hooks inspect the dispatch. The same outer dispatch keeps the active agent/session/run/tool-call context from the #83481 hook-context path.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR worktree at head `3b1bc5c4cbb25830aff01679655fd6426bb98962`, plus a clean GCP VM in `us-east1-b` using `e2-standard-4`, Node `22.22.2`, and pnpm `11.1.0`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A local after-fix probe installed a real `before_tool_call` hook runner, constructed code-mode control tools with production `createCodeModeTools()`, registered the code-mode catalog with `applyCodeModeCatalog()`, passed the outer `exec` through `splitSdkTools()`, and invoked the resulting Pi custom-tool definition with code-only, command-only, null-counterpart, hook-rewrite, and divergent-alias inputs:

```console
$ node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-83483-proof.mjs
```

Remote verification ran:

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/code-mode-control-tools.ts src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts src/agents/pi-tool-definition-adapter.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts docs/reference/code-mode.md
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/code-mode.test.ts -t 'exec-compatible code alias|divergent code and command aliases'
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts -t 'outer code-mode exec|single-alias'
$ pnpm build
```

**Evidence after fix:** The local probe produced this after-fix terminal output, redacted only by omitting unrelated setup noise:

```json
{
  "label": "code-only",
  "hookParams": {
    "code": "return 1;",
    "command": "return 1;"
  },
  "hookContext": {
    "agentId": "main",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "sessionId": "session-main",
    "runId": "run-main",
    "channelId": "channel-main",
    "toolCallId": "proof-code-only"
  },
  "result": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-before-tool-call",
    "reason": "proof block code-only"
  }
}
{
  "label": "command-only",
  "hookParams": {
    "command": "return 2;",
    "code": "return 2;"
  },
  "hookContext": {
    "agentId": "main",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "sessionId": "session-main",
    "runId": "run-main",
    "channelId": "channel-main",
    "toolCallId": "proof-command-only"
  },
  "result": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-before-tool-call",
    "reason": "proof block command-only"
  }
}
{
  "label": "null-counterpart",
  "hookParams": {
    "code": "return 4;",
    "command": "return 4;"
  },
  "hookContext": {
    "agentId": "main",
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:main",
    "sessionId": "session-main",
    "runId": "run-main",
    "channelId": "channel-main",
    "toolCallId": "proof-null-counterpart"
  },
  "result": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "deniedReason": "plugin-before-tool-call",
    "reason": "proof block null-counterpart"
  }
}
{
  "label": "single-alias-rewrite",
  "result": {
    "status": "completed",
    "value": 2,
    "output": [],
    "telemetry": {
      "visibleTools": ["exec", "wait"]
    }
  }
}
{
  "label": "divergent-alias-rejected",
  "result": {
    "status": "error",
    "tool": "exec",
    "error": "code and command must match when both are provided."
  }
}
```

The remote GCP validation produced these after-fix excerpts:

```console
Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.

✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/code-mode.test.ts (24 tests | 22 skipped) 253ms
✓ agents-support ../../src/agents/code-mode.test.ts (24 tests | 22 skipped) 253ms
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 4 passed | 44 skipped (48)

✓ src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts (17 tests | 14 skipped) 51ms
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 3 passed | 14 skipped (17)

[build-all] write-cli-compat
command complete in 7m46.376s total=8m33.443s
run summary ... exit=0
```

**Observed result after fix:** Hooks inspecting outer code-mode `exec` see both aliases populated for code-only, command-only, and malformed-counterpart inputs, while also receiving `agentId`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `runId`, `channelId`, and `toolCallId`. A hook rewrite that returns only `command` is reconciled before execution and completes with value `2`. Divergent `code`/`command` values are rejected before execution.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a live model/gateway conversation. The proof exercises the production code-mode control-tool, catalog registration, Pi custom-tool adapter, and hook runner path directly, and the remote VM ran the targeted regression tests plus `pnpm build` on the PR head.
target.author: kaspre
